title: Status, Events, Logs, Metrics, Traces, Audit, and Layered Incident Response · Object and Event Forensics
summary: An incident responder inherits a plausible but defective cluster and workload incident evidence record; preserve status and events, isolate the first causal fault in status, events, logs, metrics, traces, audit, and layered incident response, and retain a regression check. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in cluster and workload incident evidence record and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed…
objectives: Correlate spec, observedGeneration, conditions, reasons, messages, events, resource versions, controller ownership, and event retention without treating event order as durable history.
Collect current and previous container logs, multi-container streams, timestamps, kubelet and runtime logs, rotation, retention, and node-loss boundaries.
Distinguish resource metrics, component metrics, application metrics, state metrics, cardinality, scrape, storage, alert, and autoscaling evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# kubernetes-basics cumulative orchestration portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never invoke kubectl, read kubeconfig, contact a cluster API, or execute host commands.
workspace: kubernetes
version-baseline:
  kubernetes: 1.36.2
  minikube: 1.38.1
  gateway-api: 1.5.1
  helm: 4.2.2
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-cluster
evidence:

steps.title: Inspect the failure record · Debug K8s Status Conditions Events
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug workload restarts after revision 5; logs rotated, metrics aggregate away one replica, events are delayed, and an operator deletes the Pod first. Preserve object status, events, current and previous logs, metrics, traces, node evidence, and a causal timeline. Competency probe: Correlate spec, observedGeneration, conditions, reasons, messages, events, resource versions, controller ownership, and event retention without treating event order as durable history.

Inspect the K8s Status Conditions Events trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Inspect the failure record · Debug K8s Status Conditions Events.

For Inspect the failure record · Debug K8s Status Conditions Events, keep K8s Status Conditions Events connected to mechanically reviewable Kubernetes object, status, and transfer evidence.

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair · Debug K8s Layered Debugging Method
steps.instruction:
Append the Kubernetes evidence item headed "  # Evidence: k8s-layered-debugging-method-1376y4". Tailor its incident-evidence.yaml change to the current stakeholder case, retain a deterministic API or manifest review command, reject one concrete failure, and record a repair. The browser analyzes text only; run kubectl, Helm, kubeadm, and cluster commands later in an authorized disposable transfer environment.

Evidence target: Implement the cause-level repair · Debug K8s Layered Debugging Method.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns K8s Layered Debugging Method into observable performance evidence. For Implement the cause-level repair · Debug K8s Layered Debugging Method, keep K8s Layered Debugging Method connected to mechanically reviewable Kubernetes object, status, and transfer evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: k8s-layered-debugging-method-1376y4
  - competency: k8s-layered-debugging-method
    artifact: incident-evidence.yaml
    decision: layered debugging method
    environment: single-node minikube profile with a constrained Docker driver
    fault-injection: drain a node while the disruption budget has no headroom
    retained-proof: EndpointSlice DNS policy route and packet-path correlation
    case-key: 1376y4
    change: |
      correlation: release-uid-request-trace
      status: generation-and-conditions
      events: timestamped-short-retention
      logs: current-previous-and-node
      metrics: resource-system-application
      audit: identity-verb-resource
      diagnosis: first-causal-layer
    verify: kubectl get deployment,pod,endpointslice -o json && kubectl logs status-api --previous --timestamps
    failure: Reject green replica count as user proof, restart as diagnosis, events as durable history, kubectl logs as archival storage, or unbounded cardinality.
    repair: Preserve identity and time, correlate desired and observed layers, reproduce one fault cleanly, retain the regression, and document telemetry limits.
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The K8s Layered Debugging Method structural check is only one gate. cluster and workload incident evidence record must also survive the stated changed case and causal explanation.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Diagnose desired state, admission, scheduling, image, mount, process, probe, endpoint, DNS, policy, routing, storage, node, and application layers using the least invasive evidence.

hints: For Publish the incident record · Debug K8s Layered Debugging Method, name the context, namespace, API identity, desired state, observed status, controller, policy, and failure-domain boundary, K8s Layered Debugging Method precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Publish the incident record · Debug K8s Layered Debugging Method, trace this concrete input and predict admission, defaulting, scheduling, reconciliation, status, traffic, storage, policy, disruption, and cleanup evidence before reviewing it: A debug workload restarts after revision 3; logs rotated, metrics aggregate away one replica, events are delayed, and an operator deletes the Pod first. Preserve object status, events, current and previous logs, metrics, traces, node evidence, and a causal timeline. Competency probe: Diagnose desired state, admission, scheduling, image, mount, process, probe, endpoint, DNS, policy, routing, storage, node, and application layers using the least invasive evidence.

For Publish the incident record · Debug K8s Layered Debugging Method, challenge the documented misconception with object, condition, event, policy, failure, repair, rollback, and transfer evidence: Restarting or exec-editing the failing Pod is the fastest reliable root-cause method.
